filter_pushdown.index_action: skip blocks where block.max < 8766 OR block.min >= 9131; data sorted by l_shipdate so ~514/600 blocks skipped
secondary prune on surviving shipdate blocks: skip if block.max_discount < 5 OR block.min_discount > 7
combined with discount prune above: skip if block.min_quantity >= 2400

data_structures.aggregation: thread_local_int64_sum_array[nthreads] — scalar partial sums, no hash table needed; merge with simple loop
data_structures.zone_maps: raw mmap of zone map binary files, cast to struct pointers per Storage Guide layout

zone_map_usage.layout: [uint32_t num_blocks=600] then [int32_t min, int32_t max, uint32_t count] x600 (12 bytes/block)
[uint32_t num_blocks=600] then [int32_t min1(discount), int32_t max1(discount), int32_t min2(qty), int32_t max2(qty), uint32_t count] x600 (20 bytes/block)
zone_map_usage.applied_after: zone_map_l_shipdate prune — further eliminates surviving blocks

physical_plan.scan_strategy: zone_map_pruned_parallel — precompute alive block list, then parallel morsel-driven scan over alive blocks only; branchless inner loop with AVX2 auto-vectorization
physical_plan.inner_loop: branchless: pass = (d>=5)&(d<=7)&(q<2400)&(s>=8766)&(s<9131); local_sum += pass*(ep*d); enables auto-vectorization

parallelism.strategy: morsel_driven over alive blocks via atomic fetch_add on alive_blocks index
parallelism.synchronization: thread_local_partial_sum — zero contention during hot scan loop
parallelism.memory_pattern: targeted_prefetch — MADV_WILLNEED issued only for alive block pages before scan

physical_plan.output_scaling: revenue = global_sum / 10000 (both extprice and discount scaled by 100)

notes: lineitem sorted by l_shipdate => zone_map_l_shipdate prunes ~514/600 blocks (~86% skip), leaving ~86 blocks (~8.6M rows to scan)
zone_map_l_discount_qty gives secondary pruning on those 86 blocks
Branchless inner loop enables AVX2 auto-vectorization by compiler (-O3 -march=native)
Targeted MADV_WILLNEED prefetch on alive blocks only — avoids polluting page cache with dead blocks
64 threads process ~86 blocks => ~1-2 blocks per thread, minimal thread overhead
